commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: And all the angels stood round about the throne, and about the elders and the four living creatures, and fell before the throne on their faces, and worshiped God, fell: In this passage he relates, that neither the multitude, nor the living creatures, nor the elders worshipped, but the angels alone. For these are the multitude, these the living creatures and the elders. But it may also be understood of the angelic spirits themselves, of whom, as rejoicing together at the salvation of the Gentiles, it is said, "Rejoice, ye Gentiles, with His people," and , "Let all the angels of God worship Him. " <a.

commentaries.author: Bede
commentaries.content: angels: In all the angels he has represented the persons of the great multitude worshipping the Lord. "All they," he says, "who are round about Him will offer gifts."

methodology.step_1: Parse main page with BeautifulSoup for structure
methodology.step_3: Use Firecrawl to get full content from 'Go to Commentary' links
methodology.step_4: Fallback to BeautifulSoup for accordion content if needed
advantages: Records verses even without commentaries for complete tracking
